s2.ptxt: Visually check the V-ribbed belt for excessive wear, frayed cords, etc.
If any defect has been found, replace the V-ribbed belt.

atten4.ptxt: Cracks on the rib side of a V-ribbed belt are considered acceptable.
If the drive belt has chunks missing from its ribs, it should be replaced.

item.ptxt: A "new belt" is a belt which has been used for less than 5 minutes with the engine running.
A "used belt" is a belt which has been used for 5 minutes or more with the engine running.

s2.ptxt: Check that nothing gets caught in the tensioner by turning it clockwise and counterclockwise.
If a malfunction exitsts, replace the tensioner.
